location.description: MariaDB connection URI: mysql+mariadb://[user[:password]@]host[:port][/database]. If a database path is included, only that database is backed up; otherwise all databases are backed up with --all-databases.

host.description: MariaDB server hostname (default: 127.0.0.1). Overrides the host in the location URI.

port.description: MariaDB server port (default: 3306). Overrides the port in the location URI.

username.description: MariaDB username. Overrides the username in the location URI.

password.description: MariaDB password. Overrides the password in the location URI. Never appears on the command line; passed via MYSQL_PWD.

database.description: Name of the database to back up. If omitted, all non-system databases are backed up using --all-databases. Overrides the database in the location URI.

single_transaction.description: Use --single-transaction for a consistent InnoDB snapshot without locking tables (default: true). Strongly recommended for production InnoDB databases.

routines.description: Include stored procedures and functions via --routines (default: true).

events.description: Include event scheduler events via --events (default: true).

triggers.description: Include triggers (default: true). Set to false to pass --skip-triggers.

no_data.description: When true, passes --no-data so only DDL statements are dumped (no INSERT rows). Mutually exclusive with no_create_info.

no_create_info.description: When true, passes --no-create-info so only INSERT statements are dumped (no DDL). Mutually exclusive with no_data.

no_tablespaces.description: Suppress CREATE LOGFILE GROUP and CREATE TABLESPACE statements (--no-tablespaces, default: true). Recommended for restoring to managed MariaDB instances.

hex_blob.description: Dump BINARY, VARBINARY, BLOB, and BIT columns using hexadecimal notation (--hex-blob). Useful when the dump may be loaded on a system with different character set settings.

mariadb_bin_dir.description: Directory containing the MariaDB client binaries (mariadb-dump, mariadb). When omitted, binaries are resolved via $PATH.

ssl_mode.description: SSL/TLS mode for the connection (--ssl-mode). Default: not set (server decides).

ssl_cert.description: Path to the client SSL certificate file (PEM format), passed via --ssl-cert.

ssl_key.description: Path to the client SSL private key file (PEM format), passed via --ssl-key.

ssl_ca.description: Path to the CA certificate used to verify the server (PEM format), passed via --ssl-ca.
